exemplar.title:
To what extent does the migration program of the NGO Filantropija has
an impact on the integration of migrants in Slovenia?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent does the migration program of the NGO Filantropija has
an impact on the integration of migrants in Slovenia?

strengths: Logical overall structure with introduction, analysis, conclusion
weaknesses: Formatting artefacts (math symbols, incomplete sentences) interrupt flow
Inconsistent citation style and reference formatting
Awkward phrasing and missing transitions impede clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication is generally understandable but marred by formatting errors, uneven paragraphing and citation inconsistencies. This performance aligns with band 1, meriting 1/3.

strengths: Clear, focused articulation of the political issue with precise contextual grounding
Strong justification of project importance through personal and societal relevance
Engagement activities fully explained and convincingly linked to the research issue
weaknesses: Importance justification could be deepened by connecting to broader theoretical or policy frameworks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets all strands of Explanation & Justification at the top band. The issue is explicitly stated with precise context; project importance and suitability are well grounded in personal experience and stakeholder access; engagement activities are varied and directly tied back to the research focus. A score of 4/4 reflects this highâquality performance.

strengths: Identification of a clear range of appropriate secondary sources and key interviewees
Evidence of organising volunteer activities and interviews
weaknesses: No detailed timeline or rationale for source selection
Lack of scheduling details or contingency planning for engagement activities
Integration of research into engagement is intermittent rather than systematic
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Process planning is evident but remains at a basic level. Sources and activities are mostly appropriate, but there is no comprehensive schedule, rationale or feedback loop. These characteristics align with band 2, yielding 2/3.

strengths: Purposeful application of key course concepts such as soft power and structural power
Balanced, clear analysis of multiple dimensions (economic, legal, societal)
Inclusion of stakeholder testimonials and legal advocacy analysis
weaknesses: Theoretical application is not pervasive or fully integrated into every section
Synthesis of stakeholder perspectives is partial, with connections only in pockets
Analysis lacks the multiâlayered insight needed for higher bands
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work sits in the lower half of the 3â4 band. Concepts are applied accurately but not consistently. Analysis is clear and factorâfocused but not deeply layered. Synthesis is present but uneven. A score of 3/8 appropriately reflects midâband performance.

strengths: Some critical evaluation of organisational limitations (e.g., funding constraints)
Acknowledgement of research and engagement strengths and weaknesses
weaknesses: Reflection on personal bias is cursory and lacks depth
No defined criteria or metrics to evaluate engagement activities
Limited insight into future implications or changes to personal practice
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation & Reflection shows limited insight and only cursory bias awareness, placing it in band 1â2. Partial evaluation and some reflective comments justify 2/6 rather than a higher mark.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states a focused and explicitly political issue â the impact of NGO Filantropijaâs migration programme on migrant integration in Slovenia â and immediately situates it within national migration statistics, labour-market shortages and rising securitisation. Contextual details (role of Ministry of Interior, 60 587 irregular entries, definition of âmigrantâ in Slovenian discourse) demonstrate precise explanation that goes well beyond naming the topic. Hence the top band is warranted and the stronger score of 4 is justified.

Although a clear range of secondary sources and interviewees is assembled, the submission offers little evidence of a structured timeline or source-selection rationale; planning is implied rather than demonstrated. Sources are mostly appropriate, placing it comfortably in band 2 but not at the fully âwell-structuredâ level.

The student evidently organised interviews and volunteering, yet no detailed schedule, logistics chart or contingency considerations are shown. Activities appear planned enough to occur, but evidence of comprehensive, feasible scheduling is missing; therefore band 2 is the best fit.

Key GP conceptsâsoft power, structural power, national security, sovereignty, âpower-withâ, two-way integrationâare named and applied with generally accurate meaning to explain Filantropijaâs influence and constraints. Application is purposeful but not pervasive or deeply theorised, matching the lower end of the 3-4 band.

Research findings (e.g., statistics, academic discourse) are sometimes used to frame interview questions and analyse observations, but the feedback loop is irregular. Several sections rely purely on engagement testimony without secondary corroboration. This intermittent linkage aligns with âresearch sometimes informs engagementâ in band 2.

Engagements (volunteering at the Maribor centre, six stakeholder interviews, observation of language classes) are fully described and each is explicitly tied back to the research focus (e.g., volunteering to witness language-learning, interviewing government official for policy view). The strong, explicit linkage and variety merit the top of the highest band.

Language is generally clear and grammatical, but sporadic awkward phrasing, missing words and formatting artefacts impede flow in places. Meaning is only âsometimes obscuredâ, matching the descriptors for 2 marks.

The student moves beyond description by examining multiple dimensions (economic, legal, societal attitudes) and limits (funding dependence, bureaucratic hurdles) of the programmeâs impact. While analysis is not multi-layered enough for the 5-6 band, it is clear, balanced and factor-focused, justifying the top of the 3-4 band.

While the student mentions attempting to âbroaden perspective and avoid biasesâ, there is no explicit exploration of personal positionality or how preconceived views may have shaped analysis. A minimal acknowledgment places the work at the bottom of the 1-2 band (score 1).

The report follows a recognizable structure (introduction, analysis, conclusion, appendices) yet occasional digressions, long paragraphs and mixed formatting (math symbols, LaTeX debris) create some confusion. Organization therefore meets band 2 rather than the cohesive precision of band 3.

The student notes limitations of Filantropija (funding, reach) and acknowledges difficulties in assessing impact, but there is only cursory evaluation of the quality of their own research methods or engagement effectiveness. This superficial judgement meets band 1-2, with the stronger 2 awarded for at least referencing strengths and weaknesses.

The importance is linked to both personal experience (growing up in Mexico, living in Singapore) and broader relevance (NGO reliance by the state, labour shortages, rights protection). Suitability is shown by direct access to the NGO and multiple stakeholders. Justification is clear but not exceptionally sophisticated, so a solid 3 within the top band is appropriate.

Perspectives of NGO staff, government official, volunteers and beneficiaries are presented and occasionally compared (e.g., clash between NGO autonomy and state funding). However, connections are discussed in pockets rather than woven into a continuous argument, indicating partial synthesisâbest reflected by a mid-band score of 3.

The conclusion briefly states what was learned about NGO influence and calls for further research, indicating some insight, but reflection on future personal practice or deeper meta-learning is absent. This comfortably fits âlimited insightâ at the higher end of band 1-2.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly articulated and focused, providing a precise foundation for the study.
The title situates the project effectively by specifying the issue and context, aiding reader orientation.
The inline math symbol appears out of context and disrupts flow; remove or explain its relevance.
Personal background is effectively linked to the research motivation, demonstrating clear justification.
The legal analysis of advocacy fills gaps in integration discussion and adds depth.
Mention of bias is cursory; include a dedicated reflection on positionality and its influence on findings.
Inclusion of multiple testimonials enriches the synthesis of stakeholder perspectives.
Evaluation of the film festivalâs impact lacks defined criteria or metrics; specify indicators to strengthen assessment.
The conclusion should recap key findings explicitly before proposing future research directions.
Concluding remarks effectively balance acknowledgment of limitations with a statement of contribution.
Works Cited entries display inconsistent formatting and punctuation; adopt a uniform citation style.
Interviewee listing is clear but lacks rationale for selection and scheduling details, limiting transparency.
The definition of "migrant" is precise and properly cited, providing essential conceptual clarity.
The use of current migration statistics effectively grounds the context in quantitative evidence.
Volunteering description enriches insight but does not explain how those experiences informed research design.
Link program activities more explicitly to GP course concepts to deepen theoretical integration.
Discussion of labor shortages and reliance on foreign workers provides strong contextual grounding.
Primary observations of language courses enrich analysis with firsthand detail.
The paragraph ends mid-sentence, interrupting flow; complete thoughts and improve transitions.
The critique of external funding constraints demonstrates a nuanced understanding of organizational limitations.
Integrate secondary evidence to support claims about competitive grant processes and their impact.
The concept of soft power is applied effectively to frame NGO advocacy strategies.
Deepen theoretical application by linking discussion of structural power to specific GP models or frameworks.
Ensure all in-text sources are consistently formatted and complete in the reference list.

rubricData.name: Global Politics IA: Engagement Activity
rubricData.description: For the new Global Politics syllabus

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue not identified or irrelevant
2. No meaningful explanation

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: No discussion of why the project matters

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities absent or unrelated to issue

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue named but explanation vague / superficial
2. Limited or missing context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability mentioned but weak or generic

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities listed; relevance implied or weakly linked

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue explicitly stated
2. Clear, precise explanation with relevant context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability clearly justified with specific reasons

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities fully explained and convincingly linked to the issue

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: No link between research and engagement

strands.name: Engagement planning
strands.content: Activities planned but timing / logistics patchy

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: Research consistently informs engagement; findings feed back into further inquiry

strands.name: Depth of political-issue analysis
strands.content: Clear, balanced analysis; key factors explored

strands.name: Synthesis of stakeholder perspectives
strands.content: Effective synthesis; perspectives woven into coherent argument

strands.name: Awareness of personal bias
strands.content: Biases explored; impact on work examined

strands.name: Organization & structure
strands.content: Logical structure; mostly easy to follow
